title: Project Telos Tenth-Wave Cross-Domain Experiment Router Claims

claims.text: The tenth-wave arXiv intake captured 39 retained source-lead rows across five Gather stores for quantum, materials/energy, climate AI, neuroscience AI, and formal verification AI.
claims.falsification: Any named Gather store is missing or the retained catalog rows do not total 39.

claims.text: The tenth-wave source-router package explicitly maps five frontier lanes into next proof-packet candidates without treating metadata as scientific truth.
claims.falsification: The package omits the five-lane map, omits next proof-packet candidates, or promotes metadata to scientific truth.

claims.text: The tenth-wave source-router demotion gate covers all 39 retained arXiv rows and classifies them into 24 domain leads, 10 adjacent leads, and 5 query-noise rows.
claims.falsification: The demotion gate is missing, row coverage is incomplete, or the class counts differ.

claims.text: The tenth-wave package identifies duplicate source IDs across lanes and treats duplication as routing signal rather than extra paper truth.
claims.falsification: The package omits duplicate IDs or treats duplicate rows as independent truth evidence.

claims.text: The tenth-wave official-copy draft keeps quantum, climate, materials, neuroscience, theorem-replay, hardware, and wet-lab claims unverified until domain-specific proof packets exist.
claims.falsification: The draft claims a domain result, theorem replay, hardware validation, wet-lab validation, or official archive acceptance.

claims.text: The tenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries against solved-domain and exhaustive-literature claims.
claims.falsification: The content queue omits do-not-post boundaries or tells the posting session to claim solved-domain results.
